name: pdfAvaluacioCl.pdf
content:
Kids&Us.

Objectives and content of the Oliver course, 3rd term

---

Term report (3rd term)

Data

Name:                     Clémence Legrand
Teacher:                  Thérèse Garnier
Level:                    Oliver 2024/2025
Absences:                 3
Frequency of use (audio):  Insufficient

Participation/Motivation

Assessment (Participation / Motivation):   Satisfactory
Comment:
Clémence shows an acceptable level of motivation and
enthusiasm during class activities, particularly during
engaging games like the dice game. Although she encounters
challenges with vocabulary, her willingness to participate is
commendable. She attempts to interact with lesson
materials and her peers despite some struggles.
Encouragement to keep engaging positively will help her
continue to grow and develop her confidence in classroom
interactions.

Learning process

Assessment (Learning process):            Poor
Comment:
Clémence is currently facing challenges with vocabulary,
particularly words like young and old, as well as with
constructing complete sentences. While she has shown
understanding during specific activities, such as identifying
clothing for different weather conditions, consistent attention
to vocabulary is essential for her progress. To support her
learning, it is recommended that Clémence continues to
revisit vocabulary at home, helping her to build upon her
knowledge and confidence in class.

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour):                   Good
Comment:
Clémence is generally a pleasant student but occasionally
struggles to focus and stay on task. She sometimes needs
reminders to pay attention and limit conversations with her
friends during lessons. Despite these distractions, she listens
well when prompted. Her behaviour towards group activities
is friendly, and she interacts positively with her classmates.
By fostering her ability to maintain focus, Clémence can
further enhance her learning and classroom experience.

Marks

Oral Test Mark:            5.90        Needs to improve. Clémence shows a good understanding of the
                                      question, but needs to hear the sound of the first word from the
                                      teacher to provide an answer and uses only one word when
                                      answering.

---

Final comment
____________________________________________________________

Clémence has encountered several difficulties this year, particularly with vocabulary retention and attention during
class activities. While she displays enthusiasm in group settings, her distractibility hinders her progress. Moving
forward, it's crucial that she continues to practise her vocabulary over the summer. With dedicated effort, she has the
potential to improve her learning significantly.

name: pdfAvaluacioLyam.pdf
content:
Kids&Us.

Objectives and content of the Oliver course, 3rd term

---

Term report (3rd term)

Data

Name:                         Lyam Renard Meli
Teacher:                      Thérèse Garnier
Level:                        Oliver 2024/2025
Absences:                     1
Frequency of use (audio):     Insufficient

Participation/Motivation

Assessment (Participation / Motivation):    Excellent
Comment:
Lyam continues to radiate enthusiasm in class, contributing
positively to the learning environment. He eagerly
participates in all activities, demonstrating a notable
improvement in waiting his turn to speak, even when keen to
share. His comfort within the group fosters motivation,
making him enjoy lessons more. Lyam approaches every task
with readiness and a positive mindset, ensuring his
engagement levels remain high and inspiring his peers.

Learning process

Assessment (Learning process):             Excellent
Comment:
Lyam's learning has been exemplary this year. He excels at
retelling stories and has shown noticeable improvement in
making complete sentences. His ability to describe
characteristics of Oliver's family members, indicates his
understanding of the material. He quickly identifies and
corrects his mistakes, showcasing his awareness and
dedication to learning. Overall, his level of engagement is
excellent, and he enjoys demonstrating what he has learned
during class.

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour):                    Very good
Comment:
Lyam exhibits excellent behaviour in the classroom, showing
respect for his teachers and kindness towards classmates.
He enjoys participating in activities, often assisting others
when he can. His eagerness to take part is commendable,
though he needs occasional reminders to wait for his turn
to speak. Overall, his cooperative approach contributes
positively to group dynamics and the classroom environment.

Marks

Oral Test Mark:            9.90    Excellent. Lyam can understand the question and, in most cases,
                                    answers using a structure which is complete and accurate. Excellent
                                    work!

---

Final comment
____________________________________________
Lyam has had a wonderfully productive year, showcasing outstanding learning and behavioural qualities. He has made
significant strides in both his participation and his ability to articulate thoughts in complete sentences. Well done,
Lyam, and keep up this exceptional journey in learning.

name: Informes 3r trimestre Jules Petit null.pdf
content:
Kids&Us.

Objectives and content of the Oliver course, 3rd term

---

Term report (3rd term)

Data

Name: Jules Petit
Teacher: Thérèse Garnier
Level: Oliver 2024/2025
Absences: 0
Frequency of use (audio): Pass

Participation/Motivation

Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Good
Comment: Jules exhibits a generally cheerful and positive disposition in
class. Even though he can become distracted, he shows
enthusiasm during activities such as the Dice game. Jules
participates when called upon, particularly enjoying activities
like 'Who is Missing', though he requires encouragement to
engage in storytelling. His willingness to repeat after the
teacher indicates a desire to participate and learn, reflecting
his potential for increased motivation in class.

Learning process

Assessment (Learning process): Good
Comment: Jules has encountered challenges with certain concepts,
particularly with vocabulary related to clothing and sentence
structure. He has struggled with making complete sentences
and understanding prepositions, showing a need for
continued practice in these areas. Despite these hurdles,
Jules has shown some progress with vocabulary
comprehension. Encouragement and regular reinforcement
will be key to helping Jules develop his language skills
further and build his confidence in these areas.

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Good
Comment: Jules typically displays kind behaviour towards both his
classmates and teachers. He enjoys sharing materials and
playing collaborative games, showing a positive attitude when
participating. However, he sometimes becomes distracted
and has a tendency to zone out, which can affect his focus. At
times, he needs gentle reminders to refocus his attention,
especially during more challenging tasks where he tends to
retreat into his own world.

Marks

Oral Test Mark: 7.60 Good. Jules can understand the question and answers either with a
single word or with a structure which is only partially correct.

---

Final comment
____________________________________________________________
This year, Jules has shown moments of happiness and kindness, contributing positively to the classroom
environment. While he faces difficulties in maintaining focus and constructing complete sentences, he has
participated well in various activities. Looking ahead, consistent practice, especially through audio resources during
the summer, can help him realise his full potential and boost his confidence in learning next year.
